results.summary:
Hardened /Users/cracklehat/Sites/workflow-exploration/atlas-engine/disciplines/honest-verification.md as the deduped home of the no_substitution spine. Five surgical edits, structure/headers/voice preserved.

1. New section "## `no_substitution` — the spine principle (DEDUPED HOME...)" inserted right after the R2 spine paragraph. States the principle ONCE: a verifier credits only behavior observed through the slice's REAL, DESIGNATED path (true entry point, real data, real datastore/tool, in the environment the human uses for that activity); every prerequisite is front-loaded at idea-decisions.md OR surfaced as a named hard blocker; "green on a stand-in" is a TAMPER; the only autonomous-safe responses are fix-it-real or BLOCK-and-name-it. Sub-blocks:
   - no_substitution (R2 gate): R2_real_entry satisfied only via actual production entry point + real data/datastore/tool in the PROPER environment per idea-decisions.md "## Environments" (browser/user-flow R2 -> DEV env the human uses; automated tests -> TEST env with real fixtures, never reconfigured/mutated, never as-if-dev-run-in-test). Enumerates the forbidden substitutions (prod->test/CI DB swap, fake rows, mock/alternate endpoint, dropped tool/integration, switching to a working branch, convenient-env switch, click-through in test env, tests passing via env reconfig). Substitute-or-wrong-env behavior is code-verified AT BEST; BLOCKED for STANDARD/CRITICAL, never verified.
   - Blocker classification gate (required before any code-verified): external-genuinely-unavailable | config-resolvable | designated-env-broken, with a table mapping each to its allowed outcome. code-verified permitted ONLY for external-genuinely-unavailable after documenting failed resolution attempts, with committed skip-with-reason test and verdict BLOCKED/code-verified (never FAIL/infinite re-build). config-resolvable -> BLOCKED failedCriterion:R2_blocked_missing_config naming exact var(s)+how. designated-env-broken -> FAIL (fix loop) or BLOCKED failedCriterion:R2_designated_env_broken naming what broke it. Includes behavior-test-precedence note (FAIL/re-build only when R2 reachable; S4-committed test == S5 check, S5 confirms not re-derives).
   - Obstacle-ledger reconciliation: every build.md ledger entry must be fixed-with-evidence OR a named blocker; unreconciled/routed-around obstacle on a PASS is a no_substitution FAIL.

2. Three new tamper-checklist bullets appended to the existing tamper checklist: Workaround-to-green; Required-capability/substrate substitution; Wrong-environment-for-the-activity.

3. Updated the `verified` definition to require no_substitution PASS (real path + proper environment, no stand-in/wrong env).

4. Wired no_substitution into the Gate PASS line (added the AND-clause; surfaced R2_blocked_missing_config / R2_designated_env_broken as named failedCriterion examples) and added a note that everything rides on existing envelope fields + on-disk prose (build.md ledger) with no new launch-args/return fields.

Cross-file contracts respected: autonomous engine (front-load OR blocker, never ask mid-run), NO DEAD VESTIGES (routes through on-disk prose only), behavior-test precedence, ONE deliverable (S4==S5), environment scoping, and LEAN (named criteria/blockers/deliverables, no ceremony).

R2_blocked_missing_config
R2_designated_env_broken
behavior-test-present
environment-scoped
no_substitution
results.summary:
Added three named PASS sub-criteria, owned by this separate non-implementer verifier, so FAST-lane STANDARD slices are no longer builder-self-certified. All edits preserve the file's structure, headers, and voice; no new launch-args or return fields were invented (the new info routes through on-disk prose only — the §6 PASS gate, the per-criterion evidence table, and reconciliation of S4's build.md obstacle ledger / grounding note).

§6 PASS gate (lines ~164–233): added criteria 6,7,8 to the iff-ALL-hold list plus an explicit note that 6–8 are verifier-owned on every slice in both lanes, then three new sub-sections:
- §6.1 behavior-test-present: confirms the SAME persistent real-entry-point behavior test S4 committed (framed as ONE deliverable S5 confirms, not a second re-derivation) exists in changedFiles, is not skip/only/commented/empty, drives the real entry point (not a mock of the unit under test), and PASSES. Encodes the precedence: FAIL (verdict:FAIL routeBack:null, S4 fixes via fix loop) ONLY when R2 was REACHABLE (blocker-classification != external-genuinely-unavailable); when legitimately external-genuinely-unavailable the owed deliverable is the test committed skip-with-reason naming the blocker and the verdict is BLOCKED/code-verified, never FAIL; a test that FAILS/errors/was-not-written is NEVER a valid BLOCKED basis.
- §6.2 environment-scoped: each activity in its proper env per idea-decisions.md '## Environments' — browser/user-flow R2 click-through in DEV (the human's env), automated tests in TEST with its real fixtures (NOT by reconfiguring test env e.g. enabling ingest, NOT written as-if-dev but run in test). Requires recording which env EACH activity ran in (base URL/build SHA/data tier/simulator target) in the evidence table. Click-through-in-test-env, mutating/reconfiguring an env, arbitrary env-switching for convenience, and modifying the current env to pass are all a tamper + automatic FAIL. Broken DEV click-through env (incl. broken by this slice's migration/dependency): in-footprint -> FAIL routeBack:null fixNeeded; upstream -> routeBack; un-standable -> BLOCKED failedCriterion:R2_designated_env_broken.
- §6.3 no_substitution: composes honest-verification.md `no_substitution` (referenced, not restated at length) + reconciliation of S4's obstacle ledger / grounding note; substituted/side-stepped/hand-applied prerequisites are FAIL (routeBack:null in-footprint, routeBack upstream if it should have been front-loaded).

Hardened the existing BLOCKED->code-verified text (§6, ~lines 260–266): replaced the unqualified 'external dependency unavailable / environment cannot exercise the entry point' with language requiring the non-implementer verifier to INDEPENDENTLY CORROBORATE the harness is GENUINELY unrunnable with the exact missing capability named, and stating a behavior test that FAILS/errors/was-not-written is NEVER a valid BLOCKED basis -> emit FAIL.

Also updated the §7 artifact (per-criterion evidence table now lists the three criteria + per-activity environment, plus a substitution-check bullet), the §8 envelope failedCriterion enum comment (added behavior-test-present | environment-scoped | no_substitution | R2_designated_env_broken), and the §8 one-line restated PASS gate.

results.summary:
Hardened the S4 `built` gate in /Users/cracklehat/Sites/workflow-exploration/atlas-engine/steps/s4-build.md with three new named criteria plus supporting teeth, preserving structure/headers/voice.

Gate criteria added (after criterion 5):
- 6. `no-silent-workaround` — a named-in-idea-decisions external capability (CLI/credential/auth/service/account/env var/data substrate) that is unavailable or fails auth at runtime may NOT be substituted (local stub, different DB, mock, skipped integration, alternate env) and reported built. Only autonomous-safe responses: fix-it-real OR surface a hard BLOCKER with verdict:BLOCKED, failedCriterion:capability-unavailable:<capability>, exact tool/auth that failed, exact command/error, and what the human must provision. Includes the explicit ASSUMPTIONS BOUNDARY: assumptions[] is reversible-low-stakes-detail only; a worked-around capability is an obstruction or capability-unavailable BLOCKED; if unsure, BLOCK. Cites honest-verification.md no_substitution (references, does not restate).
- 7. `behavior-test-committed` — every STANDARD+/CRITICAL slice must commit >=1 persistent re-runnable test in changedFiles driving the slice's REAL entry point per the fired pack's R2 form (HTTP/UI/simulator/sample-run/CLI, not isolated unit/mock), asserting a spec-stated observable result; R1 unit tests do not satisfy it; .skip/xit/commented = absent = FAIL; not-writing-because-it-would-fail is the blocked failure. Framed explicitly as the SAME test S5 confirms (one deliverable, not two re-derivations). ONLY exception: external-genuinely-unavailable -> committed skip-with-reason naming the blocker, BLOCKED/code-verified not infinite re-build.
- 8. `declare-new-runtime-config` (config-unregistered) — a NEW env var/secret/connection string must be registered in a tracked .env.example or config manifest with name+purpose+safe local-fake/sandbox default AND recorded in the build.md Obstacle Ledger (no invented return field; S5 reads build.md). Unregistered new env read with no local-fake path = built:FAIL config-unregistered.

Supporting edits:
- Added an explanatory note that every-change-maps (criterion 2) does NOT subsume 6-8 (a workaround maps cleanly to make-the-criterion-green), so they are independent teeth.
- Added the `## Obstacle Ledger` section to the build.md artifact template: every obstacle -> exactly one of fixed-with-evidence (what broke / real fix / proving command-output) | BLOCKED-named (named blocker + what to provision); explicitly states S5 reads it and no return field exists (DESIGN §11 no dead vestiges).
- Updated §4 Test to introduce the committed R2 behavior test with environment scoping (automated tests in TEST env with real fixtures, never reconfigured; browser/user-flow R2 in DEV env per idea-decisions.md ## Environments), tied to the behavior-test-committed criterion as the same test S5 confirms.
- Updated the failedCriterion PASS line to enumerate all eight named criteria including capability-unavailable:<capability> and config-unregistered enum forms.
- Annotated the Output envelope assumptions[]/blockers[] field comments with the assumptions boundary and capability-unavailable blocker shape.

All edits respect cross-file contracts: no new launch-args/return fields (new info routed through on-disk build.md Obstacle Ledger + .env.example which S5 reads); autonomous (front-load or BLOCK, never ask mid-run); behavior-test FAIL only when R2 reachable, else committed skip-with-reason BLOCKED; one-deliverable framing between S4 and S5; environment scoping enforced.

Added the new named gate criterion `disciplines_attested` to S7 as a LEAN single spot-check (not four mirrored re-verifications), making four surgical edits to /Users/cracklehat/Sites/workflow-exploration/atlas-engine/steps/s7-verify-main-spec.md:

1. Gate `MAIN_SPEC_VERIFIED` (PASS condition): inserted `disciplines_attested` after `shipSafe`. It requires spot-checking >=1 slice/phase by citing concrete on-disk artifacts the verifier inspected itself (never an inherited phase verdict): (a) its build.md grounding note populated (WHAT EXISTS/REUSE/SIBLING not blanked); (b) its build-verification.md reached its mandatory rung (R2/R3) with a committed real-entry-point behavior test that passes — explicitly framed as the same one deliverable S4 committed and S5 confirmed, not a re-derivation; (c) IF any UI/observable surface fired then >=1 env-stamped screenshot exists whose recorded environment is the designated one from idea-decisions.md "## Environments" (DEV for browser/user-flow; convenient/wrong env = tamper). Any of (a)/(b)/(c) absent -> FAIL routeBack:S4; an existing-but-unreadable artifact -> BLOCKED.

2. FAIL branch: extended to name a build-discipline gap as a FAIL with routeBack:S4 (S4 still owns build-discipline gaps), keeping S2/S1/S0 reserved for spec/boundary/contract gaps; S7 still never edits code.

3. verification.md template: added a "## Disciplines attested" section instructing the verifier to cite the exact slice + artifact path/grep it inspected (grounding note, build-verification rung + passing behavior test, env-stamped screenshot == designated env), citing the path/grep rather than a verdict.

4. Return envelope: added "disciplines_attested" to the failedCriterion enum.

The gate intro ("iff ALL hold") and the closing PASS line ("only when MAIN_SPEC_VERIFIED holds in full") already reference the full gate, so they correctly pick up the new criterion without further edits. No new launch-args or return fields were invented; the criterion routes only through on-disk artifacts and the existing envelope fields, respecting the autonomous-engine, one-deliverable, environment-scoping, and lean contracts.
criteriaAdded: disciplines_attested
disciplines_attested (single-phase delta only)
results.summary: Made the surgical edits to s6-verify-phase.md. (1) Mirrored S7's SINGLE lean disciplines_attested spot-check into the Section 4 single-phase delta (verbatim-aligned wording: pick >=1 slice and attest by citing concrete on-disk artifacts the verifier inspected itself, never an inherited phase verdict -- (a) build.md grounding note populated WHAT EXISTS/REUSE/SIBLING not blanked; (b) build-verification.md reached its mandatory rung R2/R3 with the same committed real-entry-point behavior test S4 committed and S5 confirmed, not a re-derivation; (c) IF a UI/observable surface fired, >=1 env-stamped screenshot exists recording the designated environment from idea-decisions.md '## Environments'; any of a/b/c absent -> FAIL routeBack:S4, unreadable -> BLOCKED). This closes the escape where single-phase initiatives fold S7 into S6 and would never hit the attestation. (2) Strengthened criterion 3 slices_compose (R4) so the integrated-flow screenshot must RECORD AND VERIFY the environment it ran in -- base URL / build SHA / data tier / simulator target -- and that it is the designated env (browser/user-flow in DEV); states plainly that 'real data, not placeholder' is necessary but NOT sufficient, and an unstamped or convenient/wrong-env screenshot is a tamper. PASS line now requires the evidence artifact env-stamped with the designated environment. (3) Added disciplines_attested to gate clause 7 (single-phase only). (4) Added it to the failedCriterion enum, converting the loose 'string' field to an explicit named-criterion enum (slices_verified|phase_spec_covered|R4_compose|shared_contract_consistent|rollout_safe|tamper_audit|main_spec_covered|ship_safe|disciplines_attested|<rung> blocked) so it is never prose. (5) Added a '## Disciplines attested (single-phase only)' table to the verification.md artifact template (one row per spot-checked slice citing the exact grounding-note path, behavior-test@rung path, and env-stamped screenshot path), and added the env-stamp requirement to the R4 integration evidence line in the Artifact section. No new launch-args or engine-read return fields were invented -- all new teeth ride on the existing verification.md prose and the existing failedCriterion envelope field. Single spot-check (not four claims), consistent with the LEAN/no-ceremony constraint.

criteriaAdded: Priority Target 3 'Required build capabilities & runtime config (run-and-verify prerequisites)': mandatory enumeration, per detected surface + integration, of every tool/CLI, credential/auth, account/project access, network reachability, env-var/secret/connection-string, sandbox/test credential, and the designated verification environment the autonomous build needs to do real work AND verify R2 through the real path — each with what/why/how-to-provision and disposition (provide-now | local-fake-OK | sandbox-key-here | none-needed); names this as the only layer that can ask the human and forbids silent substitute/skip/stub

New idea-decisions.md section '## Required Capabilities & Preflight' (capability | why | provisioned?/waived-with-fallback | how-to-verify-present), with a concrete presence-probe requirement and a launch-blocker rule for any domain/external capability left no-and-unwaived

New idea-decisions.md section '## Environments' naming BOTH the DEV/click-through env (browser R2) and the TEST env (automated tests with real fixtures), per-env caveats (e.g. ingest does not run in test env), which verification runs where, with explicit prohibition on reconfiguring/test-as-if-dev/env-switching; MANDATORY when persistence/migration/external/UI surface present else repo default

New idea-decisions.md section '## Questions Asked & Answered' recording the question + literal user answer (or explicit accepted-default) per consequential decision; 'inferred-from-context' barred for any one-way-door surface

New 'Surface-lock + preflight gate (launch-blocking)' rule tied to '## Open / Blocking': BLOCKER preventing the Workflow tool call if any refine-detected surface lacks an explicit Surface Ruling/user-accepted assumption, OR any domain/external required capability is un-provisioned AND un-waived; empty/placeholder Surface Rulings with non-none Detected Surfaces = BLOCKED-not-launchable; on BLOCKED present the unresolved item as the blocking question and wait; mid-run-discovered missing prerequisite named as an un-fixable hard blocker / guaranteed degraded run
Handoff gated on the surface-lock + preflight gate being clear (## Open / Blocking = None) before launching the build
results.summary: Hardened the grill-idea front-door SKILL.md with teeth routed entirely through idea-decisions.md on-disk prose (no new launch-arg/return fields the engine ignores). (a) Added mandatory Priority Target 3 "Required build capabilities & runtime config (run-and-verify prerequisites)" enumerating per-surface/per-integration tools, creds, access, reachability, env-vars/secrets, sandbox creds, and the designated verification environment — each with what/why/how-to-provision/disposition; names grill as the ONLY layer that can ask the human and forbids silent substitute/skip/stub, with the mid-run-missing-prerequisite = un-fixable hard blocker framing. (b) Added three required idea-decisions.md sections to the Output schema with explanatory rows: "## Required Capabilities & Preflight" (capability | why | provisioned?/waived-with-fallback | how-to-verify-present, plus a presence-probe and domain/external launch-blocker rule), "## Environments" (names BOTH the DEV/click-through env for browser R2 and the TEST env for automated tests, per-env caveats like "ingest does not run in test env", which verification runs where, and bars reconfigure/test-as-if-dev/env-switch; MANDATORY for persistence/migration/external/UI surfaces else repo default), and "## Questions Asked & Answered" (question + literal answer or explicit accepted-default; inferred-from-context barred on any one-way-door surface). (c) Added a "Surface-lock + preflight gate (launch-blocking)" section tied to the existing "## Open / Blocking" line — do NOT call the Workflow tool if any refine-detected surface lacks a Surface Ruling/user-accepted assumption OR any domain/external capability is un-provisioned-and-un-waived; empty/placeholder Surface Rulings with non-none Detected Surfaces = BLOCKED-not-launchable; on BLOCKED present the item as the blocking question and wait; gated the Handoff launch on the gate being clear. Structure, headers, and voice preserved; no engine-unread fields introduced.

Replaced the unconditional "just go / skip the grill" hatch (lines 28-29) with a floored version that adds teeth without restating the deduped no_substitution principle. The new hatch permits lean-depth grill ONLY when refine found Detected Surfaces = none AND no required capability/config is un-provisioned. If ANY one-way-door surface (money|auth|data|migrations|external|PII|destructive) was detected, locking its Surface Ruling and confirming its required capabilities/config + designated environment becomes a non-skippable question before launch. It explicitly tells the user the build cannot ask later (respecting the AUTONOMOUS-engine contract — no mid-run asks; front-load in grill) and that a one-way-door surface left vague becomes an irreversible wrong assumption. The edit preserved the file's structure, the existing "assumptions will show up in the final report" voice, the step 1/2 flow, and added no new launch-args or return fields (NO DEAD VESTIGES respected — the gate routes through the grill -> idea-decisions.md path the engine already reads). No gate enum or PASS line exists in this front-door file to update.
criteriaAdded: just-go hatch is floored: lean-depth grill allowed ONLY when Detected Surfaces = none AND no required capability/config un-provisioned

Any detected one-way-door surface (money|auth|data|migrations|external|PII|destructive) makes locking its Surface Ruling + confirming required capabilities/config + designated environment a non-skippable pre-launch question
Explicit warning that the build cannot ask later and a vague one-way-door surface becomes an irreversible wrong assumption

results.summary:
Added a 5th hard question and promoted it into the `grounded` gate in /Users/cracklehat/Sites/workflow-exploration/atlas-engine/disciplines/read-before-write.md.

Edits made (surgical, preserving structure/headers/voice):

1. New question "### 5. SIBLING-CAPABILITY & CANONICAL MECHANISM" inserted after Q4. It is MANDATORY whenever the slice introduces a capability with no by-domain precedent (new ingest/upload/export/notification/integration shape), independent of whether a domain surface fired. It contrasts itself with Q2 (Q2 = helper-by-intent; Q5 = whole-capability-by-domain). Requires a by-CAPABILITY grep of the feature's action verbs + resource nouns (upload/ingest/import/parse/store + slice's own), and the verdict line `SIBLING: <path:symbol + house pattern> | none after capability search of <verbs/nouns greped>` where "none" is assertable ONLY by listing the greped verbs/nouns. For canonical process/tool surfaces (migrations -> migration CLI + dir + version table; deps -> package manager + lockfile; codegen -> config + output) requires `CANONICAL MECHANISM: <tool> — changes go through it via <command>; never hand-edit <tracked state>`, naming hand-applied DDL / hand-edited lockfiles / hand-written generated output as the silent-substitution tamper.

2. Grounding-note output block gained two lines: `SIBLING:` and `CANONICAL MECHANISM:` (the latter with an explicit "n/a — no process-managed surface fired" escape).

3. New named gate criterion `sibling-surveyed` added to `grounded`: FAILs if the capability-level search was not performed, its verdict line is absent, or a process-managed surface fired without its mechanism located; "none" assertable only by listing greped verbs/nouns. Updated the FAIL example line to include a `grounded:FAIL sibling-surveyed` case.

Consistency cleanups to avoid stale-count dead references: section header "The four questions" -> "The hard questions"; two "the four questions" references in the TARGET rule -> "the questions below"/"one of the questions below"; output-note descriptor "four-part note" -> "fact-per-line note" (since the note now has more than four lines).

No new launch-args or step-result return fields were introduced — all new info routes through on-disk prose (the grounding note inlined into the S2 plan / S4 build reasoning), consistent with NO DEAD VESTIGES. No conflict with the AUTONOMOUS engine, BEHAVIOR-TEST PRECEDENCE, or ENVIRONMENT SCOPING contracts; the no_substitution principle is referenced in spirit, not restated at length.
criteriaAdded: sibling-surveyed

results.summary:
Hardened s2-plan.md so reinventing an existing sibling capability is a hard, NAMED FAIL rather than a soft "prefer reuse." Four surgical edits, all routed through on-disk prose + existing envelope fields (no new launch-args/return fields; NO-DEAD-VESTIGES respected).

1. Reuse step (§6, lines ~100-115): Added a `follow-the-house-pattern` teeth block. When grounding's existing `SIBLING:` line (read-before-write §5) names an equivalent capability, the plan MUST either (a) reuse/extend the sibling's path/house pattern, or (b) carry a written one-line distinct-requirement argument (real divergence in contract/lifecycle/shape, not "I'd rather write it fresh"). Inventing a parallel new way with no such argument is `reuse-checked:FAIL follow-the-house-pattern`. References `no_substitution` (the deduped home in disciplines/honest-verification.md) rather than restating it.

2. Artifact template (Build plan section): Added the required plan.md line `SIBLING PATTERN: follow <path> | divergence justified: <one-line argument>` (with a "none — no sibling after capability search of <verbs/nouns greped>" form when grounding asserted none).

3. Gate criterion #6 `reuse-checked` (lines ~199-206): Folded in sub-criterion `follow-the-house-pattern` as a HARD FAIL that blocks `readyToBuild`/`readyToVerify`, carries `routeBack: null`, and is repaired by re-planning the slice onto the sibling (no new return field).

4. Gate FAIL-example exit line: Added the concrete named-criterion example `plan:ready:FAIL reuse-checked follow-the-house-pattern — sibling <path> reinvented, no divergence argument`, and noted the fix is re-planning onto the sibling.

Vocabulary aligned with the existing `SIBLING:` grounding line already emitted by disciplines/read-before-write.md §5; structure, headers, and voice preserved.
criteriaAdded: reuse-checked:follow-the-house-pattern (hard FAIL sub-criterion; blocks readyToBuild/readyToVerify, routeBack=null, fix re-plans onto the sibling)

results.summary:
Hardened the `### migrations` guardrail in web-backend.md with two new teeth, plus consistency updates to the verification clause and the Pack gate. No oneWayDoor:true was added (the migration surface is already one-way by rule), and no new launch-args or step-result fields were invented — all teeth live in on-disk prose the existing engine/verifier already reads.

Edits made:

1. New clause `0. Through-the-tool, never out-of-band` (inserted as the first check, before the existing expand/contract clause 1): the schema change must be authored AS a migration via the repo's actual migration mechanism (Prisma migrate / Rails db:migrate / Alembic / Flyway / Liquibase / Knex / TypeORM / Sequelize / Django makemigrations) and be a NEW TRACKED FILE the tool generated/recognizes. Hand-applied DDL (psql/mysql console ALTER/CREATE/DROP), editing an already-applied migration in place, or hand-editing the tool's version state (schema_migrations/_prisma_migrations/alembic_version, the migrations lockfile, generated client/schema) is a FAIL named `migration: out-of-band schema change (bypassed <tool>)`. Generalizes to codegen output and lockfile-managed deps going through their tool, never hand-edited.

2. New clause `6. Left-designated-green` (inserted after the renames clause 5, before the note): after the migration, the DESIGNATED dev DB/branch (named in idea-decisions.md "## Environments") must be confirmed RUNNABLE and the behavior verified ON IT; verifying against the test/CI DB while the designated DB stays broken is a FAIL named `migration: broke designated DB`.

3. Updated the `## verification` "Migrations in verification" bullet to require running through the migration tool (clause 0) and to require the designated dev DB left green (clause 6), so "representative copy" can't be read as license to bypass the tool or leave the designated DB broken.

4. Updated the `## Pack gate` PASS line to require every schema change went through its migration tool and left the designated dev DB green (clauses 0 + 6), and added the two new named FAIL strings to the FAIL examples enum: `migration: out-of-band schema change (bypassed <tool>)` and `migration: broke designated DB`.

Structure, headers, numbering style, and voice were preserved; environment scoping references point to idea-decisions.md "## Environments" per the cross-file contract.
criteriaAdded: migration: out-of-band schema change (bypassed <tool>)
migration: broke designated DB

results.summary:
Hardened the obstruction-loop discipline against the "route-around-a-missing-prereq" / "silently mutate tracked state" tamper by adding a 5th Channel-A declared-obstruction trigger and wiring it through every downstream gate.

Edits made to /Users/cracklehat/Sites/workflow-exploration/atlas-engine/disciplines/obstruction-loop.md:

1. §1 Channel A — added trigger 5: a missing/broken/unavailable required PREREQUISITE (env var/secret, external dependency/service, tool/integration, seed/fixture data, a migration that won't apply cleanly) OR a process-managed system changed outside its tool (hand-applied DDL/SQL, edited applied/generated migrations, hand-edited lockfile, edited codegen output instead of the canonical command). Stated it is a surface fact (named prereq/mechanism + actual error), engine-corroborable; that substituting a stand-in to get green is the no-silent-workaround failure (references disciplines/honest-verification.md no_substitution, never restated); and that the only autonomous-safe responses are fix-it-real or BLOCK-and-name-it. Also added "a named missing prereq + its error" to the surface-facts list.

2. §3 One-way-by-rule — added "any out-of-band manual change to a tracked-state mechanism" (hand-applied DDL/SQL, hand-edited applied/generated migrations or lockfiles, codegen output, or a stand-in substituted for a missing prereq) as one-way by rule, with the load-bearing sentence: an out-of-band manual change to a tracked-state mechanism is a one-way door even when it feels undoable, because it silently breaks the mechanism for later.

3. §4 Decision tree — prepended a trigger-5 branch ahead of the Tier 1 check: if the build can fix the prereq FOR-REAL in-footprint (run the canonical migration command, install the tool, write the real fixture via normal setup) -> fix it real, no stand-in, resume; otherwise (genuinely unavailable, migration won't apply, or only path left is a stand-in / hand-mutating tracked state) -> TIER 3 by default, with a routeBack iff it should have been front-loaded in grill.

4. §4 Tier 3 description — added the prereq case to the "Any of:" list and stated it is Tier 3 by default (one-way by rule) because substituting a stand-in / hand-mutating tracked state is the silent-workaround / state-corruption this catches: an unavailable prereq substituted to get green is the no-silent-workaround failure. Added "do not substitute a stand-in for a missing prereq" to the escalation imperative.

5. §4 routeBack item 3 + targetStep enum comment — a missing prereq that should have been front-loaded routes back to S0 so the grill names it in idea-decisions.md as a front-door prerequisite (naming exactly what the human must provision), respecting the autonomous/no-dead-vestiges contract (routes through on-disk prose, no new envelope field).

6. Updated the Tier 3 PASS line (tier3Escalated): added "iff prereq-should-have-been-front-loaded, routeBack targets S0 naming the prereq" and "did not substitute a stand-in or hand-mutate tracked state for a missing prereq" as gate clauses (fail -> BLOCKED, not PASS); BLOCKED failedCriterion may name the unavailable prereq.

7. §5 What you return — added a "Fix-it-real (prereq, in-footprint)" return path (run canonical command/real setup, never a stand-in, then resume) and updated the Tier 3 return line to require S0 routeBack-when-front-loadable and no stand-in / no hand-mutated tracked state.

Structure, headers, voice, and existing gate-criterion names preserved; new teeth added (named trigger, one-way-by-rule entry, decision-tree branch, PASS-clause, S0 routeBack) with no new launch-args or envelope return fields. Cross-references to honest-verification.md no_substitution are pointers only, never restated at length.
criteriaAdded: Channel-A trigger 5: missing/broken/unavailable prerequisite OR out-of-band change to a process-managed/tracked-state mechanism is a declared Obstruction (surface fact, engine-corroborable)

One-way-by-rule extended: any out-of-band manual change to a tracked-state mechanism (hand-applied DDL/SQL, hand-edited migrations/lockfiles/codegen, or a stand-in for a missing prereq) is one-way even when it feels undoable
Decision-tree branch: prereq fixable for-real in-footprint -> fix-it-real (no stand-in); else Tier 3 by default + routeBack iff should-have-been-front-loaded
routeBack to S0 names the prereq in idea-decisions.md as a front-door prerequisite (what the human must provision)
tier3Escalated PASS clause: no stand-in substituted / no tracked-state hand-mutated, and S0 routeBack naming the prereq iff it should have been front-loaded; else BLOCKED

issues.problem: Criterion 6 `no-silent-workaround` presents only two terminal responses for an unavailable capability — fix-it-real or `verdict:BLOCKED capability-unavailable:<capability>`. But disciplines/obstruction-loop.md §4 Tier 3 (and s4's own Routing-on-miss bullet 1) says a prereq that *should have been front-loaded* routes back to S0 (declare an Obstruction + routeBack:S0 naming the prereq), which is a distinct and preferable outcome to a dead-end BLOCK because it lets grill front-load it on re-launch. Criterion 6's body does point at the obstruction path ('it is an obstruction (declare it) or capability-unavailable BLOCKED'), but a builder reading the gate list in isolation could treat BLOCKED as the only sink and skip the S0 routeBack. Mildly under-connected, not contradictory.
issues.fix: In criterion 6, add one clause after the BLOCKED sentence: 'If the missing capability is one the front door should have front-loaded (a secret/tool/auth/data-substrate the designated path needs), prefer the obstruction-loop Tier-3 routeBack:S0 (§5) so grill names it next launch, rather than a terminal BLOCK.' This mirrors obstruction-loop.md:184-187 and s4 Routing bullet 1.

issues.problem: §6.2 `environment-scoped` and the §6.1 precedence both reference the blocker-classification (`external-genuinely-unavailable`, `R2_designated_env_broken`) defined in honest-verification.md, but §6.2 restates the in-footprint/upstream/un-standable routing for a broken DEV env in full prose rather than composing it from honest-verification's `designated-env-broken` table row. honest-verification.md:85 already maps `designated-env-broken` -> FAIL (in-footprint) OR BLOCKED `R2_designated_env_broken`. The s5 §6.2 restatement is consistent with it, but it is a near-duplicate of the discipline table — mild duplication against DESIGN §11 'reference a discipline module instead of restating it.'
issues.fix: Trim §6.2's broken-DEV-env routing to one line that composes the discipline: 'A broken DEV env follows honest-verification.md `designated-env-broken`: in-footprint -> FAIL routeBack:null fixNeeded; upstream -> routeBack; un-standable -> BLOCKED R2_designated_env_broken.' Keep the wrong-env tamper rule (which is s5-specific) inline.

issues.problem: The return-envelope `failedCriterion` enum (line 234-235) lists `mainspec_criteria_pass | R4_compose | tamper_audit | shipSafe | disciplines_attested | <rung> blocked`, but the gate section names the ship-safety criterion as `shipSafe` (line 138) while the per-criterion table/prose elsewhere uses `ship_safe` (snake_case) in S6 (s6:153,231,257). Within S7 it is consistently `shipSafe`; within S6 the folded-in single-phase criterion is `ship_safe`. Two different spellings for the same ship-safety gate across the two files that share the disciplines_attested mirror. Cosmetic but the engine routes on the literal failedCriterion string, so a cross-file reader/engine-author could mismatch.
issues.fix: Pick one spelling for the ship-safety criterion and use it in both files (recommend `shipSafe` in S7 to match its boolean envelope field `shipSafe:`, and note in S6 that its single-phase `ship_safe` is the same gate, or align S6 to `shipSafe`). Not load-bearing for this hardening's 9 failures, but worth aligning.
